problem.title: Case-specific Sorting of Strings
problem.description: Sort a string such that uppercase letters come before lowercase letters.
assumptions: Uppercase letters maintain relative uppercase positions; lowercase letters maintain relative lowercase positions.

example.output: "dcBA" sorted as "ABcd" but preserving case positions → "ABcd"
example.explanation: Sort uppercase letters among themselves and lowercase among themselves, keeping case positions fixed.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Two Pointers [✓ Interviewer Accepted]
solutions.description: Separate characters into uppercase and lowercase lists, sort each list independently, then reassemble by placing sorted uppercase characters into the positions that were originally uppercase and sorted lowercase into the original lowercase positions. This preserves the case structure of the input while sorting each case group.
